question.text: If REIT units are not listed or money is not refunded within the specified time, what rate of interest per annum must the REIT pay?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under REIT Regulations, REITs shall pay interest at 15% per annum from the closure of the issue in case the Units are not listed or the money refunded within the specified time. **The Trap:** This is the same 15% rate as for InvITs. Both frameworks impose the same penalty rate for delayed listing or refund. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 15% penalty interest rate is consistent across InvITs and REITs, serving as a strong deterrent against listing or refund delays.
